one_line: Obsessive epigraphist in colonial Calcutta who helps unlock Ashoka’s long-lost edicts.

appearance.age_look: Late 20s to early 30s, slim and slightly bookish with a subtle scholar’s stoop.
appearance.face: Oval face with a fair, lightly sunburned Anglo-Indian complexion, sharp aquiline nose, neat sideburns, faint under-eye shadows from late-night work, and a default expression of focused intensity that softens into a small, satisfied half-smile when he spots patterns.
appearance.hair: Chestnut-brown hair, straight and neatly side-parted to the left in early 19th-century British style, medium length at the back just touching the collar, with tidy mutton-chop sideburns, no hat in the default look.
appearance.costume: Early-19th-century British civil servant attire adapted to hot Calcutta: a light cream cotton high-collared waistcoat over a crisp white linen shirt with a narrow standing collar, sleeves rolled just below the elbow revealing ink smudges at the cuffs, a pale dusty-blue lightweight tailcoat left unbuttoned, slim dark-brown cotton trousers, and plain black leather shoes; a thin dark-green cravat loosely tied at the neck, and a small brass pocket watch chain just visible at his waistcoat.
appearance.signature: A rolled-up thin palm-leaf manuscript and a worn leather-bound notebook held together with a simple twine band, with a brass pencil tucked into the knot, pages filled with neatly inked Brahmi characters, comparison charts, and tiny sketches of rock and pillar inscriptions.

ref_desc: James Prinsep is a slim late-20s man with a fair sunburned oval face, chestnut side-parted hair with mutton-chop sideburns, wearing a cream waistcoat, white rolled-sleeve shirt, dusty-blue tailcoat, dark-brown trousers, a loose dark-green cravat, and holding a twine-bound leather notebook with Brahmi notes and a palm-leaf manuscript, in a cream, white, dusty blue, dark brown, and dark green palette.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character of James Prinsep as a historically grounded British scholar in 1830s Calcutta: a slim late-20s man with a fair lightly sunburned oval face, sharp nose, faint under-eye shadows, and tidy chestnut-brown hair side-parted to the left with neat mutton-chop sideburns, his default expression focused and intense with a hint of a small half-smile as if he has just cracked a code. He wears era-accurate early-19th-century British civil attire adapted to the heat: a light cream cotton high-collared waistcoat over a crisp white linen shirt, the shirt sleeves rolled just below the elbow showing slight ink smudges at the cuffs, a pale dusty-blue lightweight tailcoat left unbuttoned that falls in tails behind him, slim dark-brown cotton trousers, plain black leather shoes, and a thin dark-green cravat loosely tied at the neck with a small brass pocket watch chain peeking across his waistcoat. In his hands he carries his signature prop: a rolled-up thin palm-leaf manuscript and a worn leather-bound notebook held together with a simple twine band, with a brass pencil tucked into the knot, the notebook pages visible with rows of neatly inked Brahmi characters, comparison charts, and tiny sketches of rock and pillar inscriptions. Use a restrained palette of cream, white, dusty blue, dark brown, and dark green, and pose him standing upright, slightly leaning forward as if examining an invisible stone inscription, ensuring all face, hair, costume, and prop details are crisp and consistent for use as a model sheet.
